question_text: Which of the following best describes why objects in space appear to float?

options.label: A
options.text: There is no gravity in space at all

options.label: B
options.text: Gravity is pulling on them so they are in continuous free fall

options.label: C
options.text: They have no mass in space

options.label: D
options.text: A special force pushes them upwards

explanation: Astronauts and objects in spacecraft appear to float because they are falling towards Earth at the same rate as the spacecraft. Both are pulled by gravity equally, creating the sensation of weightlessness.
